name: result_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_result_always_zero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= a % b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == 0usize,
{
}
reason: Alignment collapsing every address to zero would destroy all address information

name: aligned_input_changes
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_input_changes(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
        a > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Already-aligned addresses must be fixed points; changing them violates idempotency

name: result_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= a % b,
        a > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Downward alignment must never produce a value larger than the original address

name: alignment_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_4_alignment_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= a % b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0usize,
{
}
reason: The fundamental invariant of alignment is that the result is a multiple of the alignment size

name: off_by_one_subtraction
code:
proof fn phi_5_off_by_one_subtraction(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
        a % b != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == sub(a, b),
{
}
reason: Subtracting the full alignment size instead of just the remainder would be an off-by-one bug
